text: Moving from a quiet, almost sterile hallway into a space covered with vibrant colors and the promise of innovation, I am struck by the contrast. The hallway felt contemplative; it invited a certain solitude, arousing curiosity mixed with a tinge of uncertainty as if on the brink of the unknown. Entering the exhibition, surrounded by the playful energy of R+D and glimpses of human creativity, I feel a surge of excitement. In this vibrant intersection of solitude and collaboration, I am reminded of the perpetual tension and harmony between introspection and progress. It is invigorating, slightly disorienting, and deeply thought-provoking.
